items.title: US500 Index Rises to 7,505 Points
items.content: US500 rose to 7,505 points on July 21, gaining 0.83%. Up 18.95% year-over-year.

items.title: Major Indexes Close Lower; Oil Rises as Iran War Intensifies
items.content: Stocks failed to sustain early gains. Oil rose amid escalating Iran conflict. Dow had previously crossed 53,000 record.

items.title: Dow Jumps to Record Closing High After Soft US Jobs Data
items.content: Dow hit record close after weaker-than-expected June jobs data, while Nasdaq fell on chip share weakness.

items.title: Hang Seng Surges 2.36% to 25,143
items.content: Hong Kong stocks rallied sharply on Monday with HSI closing up ~581 points (+2.36%) at 25,143.05, led by offshore Chinese equities.

items.title: Hang Seng Opens Lower as Technology Shares Decline
items.content: HSI opened down 153 pts (-0.61%) at 24,978 on Wednesday, dragged by tech stock weakness.

items.title: Wholesale Inflation June 2026: PPI Falls 0.3%
items.content: Producer Price Index dropped 0.3% in June (below consensus of unchanged). Annual PPI inflation at 5.5%.

items.title: US CPI June 2026 Falls to 3.5%, Below Forecast
items.content: Headline CPI rose 3.5% YoY in June, below consensus of 3.8%. Energy prices saw sharp monthly decline of -0.4%.

items.title: US and Iran Agreed Ceasefire on April 7-8 After 5 Weeks of Fighting
items.content: After more than five weeks of conflict, US and Iran agreed to ceasefire. Strait of Hormuz reopening is a stated US priority.

items.title: Trump Declared Iran MOU 'Over' on July 8 Amid Renewed Hormuz Strikes
items.content: On July 8, Trump declared MOU with Iran 'over' after Iran allegedly struck more targets in Strait of Hormuz. Renewed tensions.

items.title: China AI Chip Stocks Rally After Z.AI's Domestic-Chip Data Center Milestone
items.content: Chinese AI and semiconductor stocks rallied after Bloomberg reported Z.AI completed a large data center powered entirely by domestic chips.

items.title: Chinese Mainland Stocks Advance on AI, Chip Rally; HK Shares Slip
items.content: Chinese mainland stocks led by AI, semiconductor and metal shares gains. Hong Kong equities declined as investors booked profits.
